channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Here are 38 Memorial Day deals we recommend for $50 or less
item.description: Massive OLED TVs and Sonos speakers might be stealing the Memorial Day spotlight, but there are also plenty of great deals that won’t set you back nearly as much. In fact, some of the best...

item.title: # I Built an AI Website Builder and Here's What Actually Happened
item.description: I've been wanting to build something with AI for a while now. Not just a wrapper around ChatGPT, but something that actually feels useful. So I built CrafticWeb — you describe a website, and it...

item.title: Cooking an AI Campaign in 5 Minutes with Google Cloud AI APIs
item.description: About a month or so ago, during the Build with AI season, Google Developer Groups Johannesburg in South Africa hosted a build-a-thon with a concept I genuinely loved. The whole concept was...

item.title: Your PM Retrospectives Are Lying to You
item.description: Every quarter, product teams hold retrospectives. Someone asks "what went well?" and "what didn't?" The team lists bullet points. Someone writes "we should communicate better." Everyone nods....

item.title: How I Built a Free, Self-Hosted Pipeline That Auto-Generates Faceless YouTube Shorts
item.description: Every "AI YouTube" tutorial ends the same way: sign up for ChatGPT Plus, then ElevenLabs, then Pictory, then n8n Cloud. Add it up and you're paying $75–100/month before you've made a single video...

item.title: TypeScript 54 to 58: The Features That Actually Matter in 2026
item.description: TypeScript 5.4 to 5.8: The Features That Actually Matter in 2026 I've been tracking TypeScript releases for 3 years. Most release notes are noise — "improved inference in corner cases" doesn't...

item.title: How to Tailor Your CV to Any Job Posting in 2026
item.description: Most job seekers spend more time refreshing their inbox than they do tailoring their CV to the job posting in front of them. That's a mistake that costs more interviews than any skill gap ever...

item.title: 95. Fine-Tuning LLMs: Make a General Model Do Your Specific Job
item.description: A general language model knows a little about everything. It knows some medicine. Some law. Some code. Some cooking. But it doesn't know your specific domain deeply. It doesn't know your company's...

item.title: What Is a Frontend Developer Roadmap and Why You Need One
item.description: Every year, thousands of people decide to become frontend developers. They open YouTube, type "learn HTML," and dive in with genuine enthusiasm. A few weeks later, many of them are stuck - not...

item.title: Google shipped three Gemini "Flash" models. Picking the wrong one could 6 your AI bill
item.description: This is a submission for the Google I/O 2026 Writing Challenge Google shipped three Gemini "Flash" models. Picking the wrong one could 6× your AI bill. I opened Google AI Studio right after the...

item.title: Building an MCP server so Claude can query my SaaS analytics directly
item.description: Last week I shipped a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for my analytics SaaS. Now Claude Desktop, Cursor, and any MCP compatible client can query traffic, revenue, and funnel data directly....

item.title: Elon Musk has given up on solar power (on Earth)
item.description: Elon Muks's xAI has gone all in on natural gas, while SpaceX is obsessed with orbital data centers. What happened to the "solar-electric economy" he promised?

item.title: Hanging out in my favorite virtual coffee shop in Tokyo
item.description: Finding a cafe that fits you can be a revelatory experience. For me at least, there are few places outside of my house that I can truly feel comfortable in. I'm lucky enough to have two options in...

item.title: I have a new go-to browser
item.description: Hi, friends! Welcome to Installer No. 129, your guide to the best and Verge-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, come on you Gunners, and also you can read all the old editions...

item.title: Claude Mythos AI Finds 10,000 High-Severity Flaws in Widely Used Software
item.description: Anthropic on Friday disclosed that Project Glasswing has helped uncover more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerabilities across some of the most "systemically" important software across...

item.title: Google’s new anything-to-anything AI model is wild
item.description: Last year I deepfaked my kid's stuffed animal to make it look like his plush deer was on vacation. It was an experiment to see if I could re-create the events depicted in a Gemini ad Google was...

item.title: Laravel-Lang PHP Packages Compromised to Deliver Cross-Platform Credential Stealer
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh software supply chain attack campaign that has targeted multiple PHP packages belonging to Laravel-Lang to deliver a comprehensive...

item.title: LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin CVE-2026-48172 Exploited to Run Scripts as Root
item.description: A maximum-severity security vulnerability impacting LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin has come under active exploitation in the wild. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-48172 (CVSS score: 10.0), relates...

item.title: Drupal Core SQL Injection Bug Actively Exploited, Added to CISA KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a recently patched critical security flaw impacting Drupal Core to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog,...

item.title: Towards Speed-of-Light Text Generation with Nemotron-Labs Diffusion Language Models
AI is being used to resurrect the voices of dead pilots
item.description: People used AI on a spectrogram image of cockpit recordings to reconstruct them, forcing the NTSB to temporarily block access to its docket system.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: Regulating Squid Fishing in the South Pacific
item.description: The South Pacific Regional Fisheries Management Organization (SPRFMO) needs to regulate squid fishing in the South Pacific. As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security...

item.title: Google goes for the glitter with disco-ball icons: ‘Are y’all sure you still want this?’
item.description: You can now disco ball-ify your entire Pixel home screen, says Google.

item.title: How VCs and founders use inflated ‘ARR’ to crown AI startups
item.description: Some AI startups are stretching traditional revenue metrics when talking about progress publicly. And their investors are fully aware.

item.title: Google’s AI search is so broken it can ‘disregard’ what you’re looking for
item.description: Google's AI Overviews are running into an interesting problem right now. Earlier on Friday, if you searched for the term "disregard," the AI Overview section would include a response like what...

item.title: Elon Musk can’t hear you over the sound of his $1.75 trillion IPO
item.description: The SpaceX S-1 is finally here, and the story it tells goes way further than rockets. The filing runs to 36 pages of risk factors alone, and the numbers inside match the ambition: a $28 trillion...

item.title: Texas AG sues Meta over claims that WhatsApp doesn't provide end-to-end encryption
item.description: Critics note a lack of factual support in lawsuit filed by US Senate candidate.

item.title: Catch up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026.

item.title: Twelve South’s AirFly Pro 2 has hit one of its best prices ahead of summer travel
item.description: With Memorial day weekend kicking off the travel season, we’re seeing a lot of deals pop up on travel gadgets, from portable power banks to noise-canceling headphones. One of the best right now is...

item.title: First VPN Dismantled in Global Takedown Over Use by 25 Ransomware Groups
item.description: Authorities in Europe and North America have announced the dismantling of a criminal virtual private network (VPN) service used by criminal actors to obscure the origins of ransomware attacks,...
